reference_descriptions.technical_architecture: Infrastructure components (API gateway, cache, database, message queue)
reference_descriptions.endpoints: API endpoint definitions for fleet, mission control, robot commands, and telemetry
reference_descriptions.libraries: Code libraries for HTTP client, state management, UI components, validation, WebRTC
reference_descriptions.protocols: Communication protocols for authentication, communication, and telemetry formats
reference_descriptions.data_models: Detailed model schemas for maintenance, missions, parts, robots, telemetry, users
reference_descriptions.platforms: Platform specifications for AWS infrastructure, mobile, and web platforms
reference_descriptions.business_logic: Detailed workflows and rules for operations like emergency stops, mission assignment, telemetry processing
reference_descriptions.acceptance_criteria: Success criteria for features with functional, performance, and integration requirements
reference_descriptions.state_mutations: State change specifications for critical operations
reference_descriptions.labels: Specific text labels for buttons, fields, and UI elements (e.g., emergency-stop-btn)
reference_descriptions.styles: Specific CSS styles for UI elements (e.g., primary-btn, glass-overlay)
reference_descriptions.colors: Color values with semantic names (e.g., error, primary, bg-dark)
reference_descriptions.spacing: Spacing arrays for micro, component, and layout spacing
reference_descriptions.typography: Font stack, sizes array, and weights array
reference_descriptions.breakpoints: Responsive design breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop, wide)
reference_descriptions.z-index: Z-index layers for consistent stacking (dropdown, modal, tooltip, notification)
reference_descriptions.assets: Visual assets including images, icons, and media files for UI components and content
reference_descriptions.api_contracts: Detailed request/response schemas, error codes, rate limits for all API endpoints
reference_descriptions.validation_rules: Field-level and cross-field validation specifications for data integrity
reference_descriptions.error_states: Error messages, recovery actions, and fallback behaviors for user-facing errors
reference_descriptions.performance_specs: Response time SLAs, data limits, concurrency requirements, and load specifications
reference_descriptions.notifications: Alert rules, message templates, delivery channels, and notification triggers
reference_descriptions.configuration: Feature toggles, environment configs, deployment settings, and runtime parameters
reference_descriptions.metrics: Analytics events, KPIs, success metrics, and tracking requirements for product insights
reference_descriptions.examples: Sample inputs/outputs, edge cases, test scenarios, and happy path demonstrations
reference_descriptions.constraints: Business rules, system-wide invariants, and resource limits that must always hold
reference_descriptions.terminology: Domain-specific terms, abbreviations, naming conventions, and industry jargon
reference_descriptions.sequences: Order of operations, initialization sequences, shutdown procedures, and event cascades
reference_descriptions.mappings: Transformations between representations, status codes to messages, and field mappings
reference_descriptions.defaults: System-wide default values, fallback behaviors, and initial states for entities

reference_notes.purpose: References are terminal graph nodes used for template generation and detailed specifications
reference_notes.schema: Each reference category uses a flat, but flexible schema tailored to its specific needs
reference_notes.naming: Reference keys use plaintext naming (not key_object_notation) for final nodes
reference_notes.usage: Entities in the graph can reference these nodes for implementation details and specifications

meta_descriptions.workflows: Multi-step user journeys and collections of actions that form complete processes
meta_descriptions.assumptions: Explicit list of technical and business assumptions underlying the design
meta_descriptions.phases: Project implementation phases with dependencies and parallelization information
meta_descriptions.out_of_scope: Features and capabilities explicitly excluded from the project
meta_descriptions.deployment: Current deployment status and rollout information for different features
meta_descriptions.project: High-level project information including name, tagline, and brand promise
meta_descriptions.decisions: Architecture and design decisions with rationale, alternatives considered, and trade-offs

entity_descriptions.project: Top-level container representing the entire software project or system
entity_descriptions.requirements: Functional or non-functional specifications that the system must satisfy
entity_descriptions.interfaces: User interface screens and pages in the application
entity_descriptions.features: Distinct functionality or capability provided to users
entity_descriptions.actions: Specific user interactions or system operations
entity_descriptions.components: Reusable building blocks of the system architecture
entity_descriptions.presentation: Visual and layout aspects of user interface components
entity_descriptions.behavior: Logic and state management for component interactions
entity_descriptions.data_models: Structure and schema definitions for data entities
entity_descriptions.users: Actors or roles that interact with the system
entity_descriptions.objectives: High-level goals or outcomes that users want to achieve

entity_notes.schema: Entities only have name & description keys

notes.names: reference and entity keys use snake case, use - for keys, not _
notes.schema: Do not use id, the parent key is the unique identifier
